source: Hadolint, Docker best practices, CIS Docker Benchmark

rules.name: missing-user-instruction
rules.message: No USER instruction found — container will run as root, increasing attack surface.
rules.fix_hint: Add a non-root user and set USER before CMD/ENTRYPOINT. Example: RUN adduser -D appuser && USER appuser
rules._note: Structural check — detected via awk scanning for USER instruction, not regex pattern matching.

rules.name: latest-base-image
rules.message: Base image uses :latest tag — builds are not reproducible and may break unexpectedly.
rules.fix_hint: Pin to a specific version or digest. Example: FROM node:22-alpine or FROM python:3.12-slim

rules.name: add-instead-of-copy
rules.message: ADD used instead of COPY — ADD has implicit tar extraction and remote URL fetch which can be surprising.
rules.fix_hint: Use COPY for local file copies. Reserve ADD only for tar auto-extraction or remote URLs (prefer curl/wget for URLs).

rules.name: apt-get-no-recommends
rules.message: apt-get install without --no-install-recommends — installs unnecessary packages, bloating image size.
rules.fix_hint: Add --no-install-recommends flag. Example: RUN apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl

rules.name: curl-pipe-to-shell
rules.message: curl piped to shell — executes remote code without verification, risking supply-chain attacks.
rules.fix_hint: Download the script first, verify its checksum, then execute. Or use the package manager instead.

rules.name: private-key-block
rules.message: Private key detected in source code
rules.fix_hint: Remove private key from source. Use key management service or environment variable.

rules.name: connection-string-credentials
rules.message: Connection string with embedded credentials detected
rules.fix_hint: Use environment variables for connection strings. Never embed passwords in URIs.

file_size._note: Lines of code. Dockerfiles should be concise; multi-stage builds get more room.

function_size._note: Not applicable to Dockerfiles. RUN instruction complexity is checked via rule patterns.
